organizationUrnResolutionResult.headquartersLocation: Haifa, Haifa District, Israel
organizationUrnResolutionResult.description:
Approximately 36 years before Israel declared independence, a ceremony took place on the barren slopes of Mount Carmel near the Port of Haifa, which was then occupied by the Ottoman Empire. Unknown to the Haifa community witnessing the event, this would be a milestone in history. This first cornerstone embodied an implausible vision of creating a world-class institute of scientific and technological education in the Holy Land.
The story of the “Technikum” – the original German name of the Technion – is a tale of the century. The second industrial revolution created the printing presses and communications infrastructure allowing Jews scattered around the globe to organize in face of rising anti-Semitism. As Jews were often barred from technical education, the establishment of a technical school was a first priority to rebuilding a Jewish homeland. The Technion was to become a unique worldwide university that would precede, create, shape, and protect a modern state.
The cornerstone laid on April 11, 1912, set in motion a century of progress responding to national and global needs. Technion – Israel Institute of Technology would grow rapidly, becoming a global pioneer in fields such as biotechnology, stem cell research, space, computer science, nanotechnology, and energy. 
Four Technion professors have won Nobel Prizes. As it approaches its centennial in 2025 – 100 years after the first class took place – Technion City is a thriving world center of trailblazing excellence in research and teaching for the benefit of humanity, with 18 faculties, more than 14,000 students, 135 academic programs, and 60 research institutes. Since the Technion opened its doors, more than 90,000 Technion alumni have played a huge part in creating and leading the ‘Startup Nation,’ impacting the lives of millions of people worldwide.
organizationUrnResolutionResult.name: Technion - Israel Institute of Technology

organizationUrnResolutionResult.headquartersLocation: Tel Aviv-Yafo, Tel Aviv District, Israel
organizationUrnResolutionResult.description:
Academic excellence and contribution to the local community have been at the heart of the College's vision since its establishment. The Academic College of Tel Aviv-Yafo was founded in 1994 as a non-profit public institution, and in under three decades has made substantial progress in realizing its vision.
 
The College's six schools offer nine Bachelors programs and ten Masters programs. 4,780 students are currently enrolled in the College, which already boasts nearly 19,000 graduates. Our alumni have amassed significant achievements in academia and business, as well as civic activities and public initiatives. 
 
The schools of Management & Economics, Computer Sciences, Behavioral Sciences, Nursing Sciences, Government & Society, and Information Systems offer students a range of study programs that encourage inspiring and collaborative dialog among various disciplines.
 
The College’s curricula are rigorous and challenging, and the faculty is committed to high quality teaching and active, ongoing research. The College's "open door" policy helps students cope with both the demanding study requirements as well as with personal problems.

organizationUrnResolutionResult.description:
ORT colleges are part of ORT Israel, which was established in 1949 and now includes 150 educational institutions. The network trains students to cope with the accelerated rate of development in the economy and in industry.

ORT colleges devise study curricula adapted to the needs of the country and to future needs, in collaboration with other bodies, such as: the Ministry of Science and Technology, the Ministry of Industry, Trade & Employment, the IDF, high-tech industries and other leading industries in the national economy, both in the private and public sectors.

Our graduates are integrated in the employment market in a wide range of key positions, in high-tech industries, other leading industries, government ministries, in the private market and in a large number of places of employment that require high quality professionals in the technological fields, at all levels.

ORT colleges operate as interdisciplinary education centers, ranging from science and technology studies to the arts, design and communications. The colleges aim to provide a solution for every student, enabling them to successfully meet local and global technological requirements.

organizationUrnResolutionResult.description:
Reichman University (previously IDC Herzliya), the first private institution of higher education in Israel, opened its doors in 1994 thanks to the vision of Prof. Uriel Reichman. Reichman University's ten schools offer innovative, dynamic undergraduate and graduate programs that educate Israel's future business, juridical, high-tech, communications, economics, and civic leaders. 
All of the academic programs provide students with both a solid theoretical background and the practical interdisciplinary skills that are essential for success in the 21st century. 

The university’s alumni are leading key positions at government agencies, high-tech companies, and financial institutions. 

Reichman University is unique in its educational methods, which are based on an interdisciplinary approach and the teaching of information technology and global markets. It combines theory with real-world experience and provides its students with proficiencies. 

Our basic outlook, which is rooted in the twin concepts of individual freedom and responsibility, emphasizes student entrepreneurship and leadership alongside a commitment to community service.

Follow us on all social media channels >>> https://bit.ly/3TVTCJE

organizationUrnResolutionResult.description:
HackerU is a leading Techcelerator dedicated to training the next generation of the high-tech industry. 
We provide comprehensive services for individuals seeking to make a career change and enter the Israeli high-tech sector. Our all-in-one solution includes personalized guidance, screening, training, and job placement assistance. With over 30 years of experience, we excel at identifying candidates' potential, matching them with suitable fields, and providing top-notch professional training.
As a part of TechDX group, we are spearheading the Tech Education revolution globally, delivering high-quality training programs in various institutions such as universities, government bodies, armies, and police forces. We offer training in over 14 different fields, including programming, information and cyber security, digital marketing, graphic design, gaming, and 3D animation. Our flexible morning/evening study formats range from 4 to 14 months, and prior experience in the field is not necessary for acceptance. We believe in equalizing opportunities through education and strive to make high-tech training accessible for all individuals who possess the motivation and desire to integrate into the industry.

organizationUrnResolutionResult.headquartersLocation: Israel
organizationUrnResolutionResult.description:
In the academic environment in Israel, the Open University of Israel is similar to other universities in its pursuit of excellence and its commitment to superior scientific and scholastic standards. However, it differs in its organizational structure, its curriculum, and its admission requirements. 

The Open University is open to all those who wish to study a single course or a battery of courses, or to pursue a full program of study toward a Bachelor's degree. Enrollment does not require matriculation or any other certificate from another educational institution. Though applicants are not required to provide proof of prior scholastic achievements, their academic achievements are the key to their success at the Open University. Along with its open admission policy, the Open University demands of its students personal qualities without which academic achievements cannot be attained: ambition, motivation, perseverance, responsibility and self-discipline. Students are required to study on their own, practice at home, actively participate in group learning activities, and meet course deadlines and requirements. 

Since 1996, the Open University has offered programs of study leading to a Master's degree. In contrast to the open admission policy for undergraduate studies, admission to graduate studies is contingent on fulfillment of certain requirements. 
The Open University is flexible in structure and organization. Applicants joining the Open University student body are not required to enroll in a faculty or department, but rather in specific courses. An individual enrolled in a course becomes a student of the Open University. 

The structure of studies at the Open University also differs from the structure customary at other universities, as it is not defined by years. Open University students are not first, second or third year students. Student are entitled to determine their own rate of progress and the degree of progress in not measured by units of time ...
organizationUrnResolutionResult.name: The Open University of Israel

companyResolutionResult.description:
Founded in 1935, Israel Discount Bank is a prominent financial institution with a rich history, establishing itself as a leading banking corporation in the region.

Discount Bank caters to diverse customer segments, including households, small and mid-sized businesses, large corporations and institutes, and private banking offering a wide range of high-quality banking services tailored to each segment.

Innovation and customer satisfaction are central to Discount Bank's mission, reflected in services such as a user-friendly website, SMS services, and a unique "Mafteah Discount" credit card that rewards home and business purchases.

Discount Group operates abroad through IDB New York, the largest Israeli Bank operating overseas with branches in the New York area, Florida and California, focusing on the business-commercial sector and private banking.

 Holdings include Mercantile Discount Bank (MDB), specializing in services for small businesses, households, and local authorities. Discount Capital, Tafnit Discount Asset Management, and PayBox are integral components of the Discount Group, enhancing the array of financial solutions provided.

Discount Bank is deeply committed to community involvement and supports social mobility through education and work initiatives, positively impacting Israeli society. The bank actively collaborates with FinTech companies to deliver cutting-edge financial services tailored to customer needs.

Discount Bank's strategic plan for 2021-2025 focuses on solidifying its position as a top-tier financial institution, delivering value to both shareholders and customers. At its core, Discount Bank remains dedicated to community involvement and significantly contributes to social and cultural projects, aligning with its commitment to creating a positive impact on society.
companyResolutionResult.name: Discount Bank בנק דיסקונט

currentPositions.description:
I manage Coders Clan IL, one of the largest and most active tech communities in Israel, bringing together hundreds of developers, engineers, and tech enthusiasts. Our mission is to create a collaborative space where juniors and seniors can learn from each other, share knowledge, and grow through hands-on experience.

I lead the community’s growth and activities—organizing meetups, building engaging content, driving online discussions, and supporting members in their professional journeys. Beyond learning, Coders Clan is about connection: helping developers find opportunities, work together on projects, and feel part of a vibrant ecosystem.

Together with a passionate core team, we’re building an inclusive, supportive environment where people can sharpen their skills, collaborate across disciplines, and have fun while doing it.

organizationUrnResolutionResult.description: המרכז האקדמי פרס הינו מוסד ייחודי בנוף האקדמיה הישראלית, המוביל במצוינות אקדמית ובליווי הסטודנטים באופן אישי עד הקריירה. המרכז מציע תוכניות לימוד גמישות וייחודיות, סגל אקדמי מעולה, ושם לעצמו מטרה להוות בית חם לכ-7,000 הסטודנטים הלומדים בו. בקמפוס הייחודי, שמספק את הציוד והכיתות החדישים ביותר שיש, תמצאו סגל אקדמי מהשורה הראשונה, מסלולים המלווים ע”י בכירי החברות במשק, תעודות מקצועיות שניתנות במהלך התואר האקדמי, תוכניות למצטיינים ועוד ועוד. במרכז תוכניות לימוד מגוונות, המותאמות אישית לצרכיו של הסטודנט הלומד, כך שאם יבחר, יוכל להמשיך ולפתח את הקריירה כבר במהלך הלימודים.

9,000   students | 85,000 alumni | Diverse Bachelor's and Master's programs

companyResolutionResult.description: Tipalti is the AI-powered platform for finance automation, elevating how finance teams operate in the global economy. We empower our customers to scale faster and smarter by removing the complexities of doing global business and accelerating their finance operations efficiency. Our platform provides a comprehensive suite of finance automation solutions designed for mid-market businesses across accounts payable, global payouts, procurement, employee expenses, corporate cards, supplier management, tax compliance, and treasury. Tipalti partners with leading financial institutions such as Citi, Wells Fargo, J.P. Morgan, and Visa, enabling over 5,000 global companies to efficiently and securely pay millions of suppliers and payees across 200+ countries and territories, in 120 currencies. For more information, visit tipalti.com.

companyResolutionResult.description:
Financial institutions are typically torn between two bad options: Either legacy systems constrain their ability to deliver on digital initiatives, or they enter modernization programs that cost $300M+, take 5+ years and fail >70% of the time.

Tweezr addresses both scenarios - it is an AI-powered surgical code assistant that unlocks developer productivity in legacy environments, by pinpointing exactly where they need to make changes across 10Ms lines of code without breaking critical functionality.

companyResolutionResult.description:
Connecting the dots between cloud, Kubernetes, container, and application security. 

Zero-day and every day protection for your cloud applications with the first runtime behavioral Cloud Application Detection and Response (CADR) solution.

A fully explainable & traceable runtime security story spanning the entire cloud technology stack.

𝗖𝗔𝗗𝗥: Complete protection for your apps from cloud to code
Threat detection and automatic response based on anomaly analysis powered by our eBPF sensor.

𝗩𝗨𝗟𝗡𝗘𝗥𝗔𝗕𝗜𝗟𝗜𝗧𝗬 𝗠𝗔𝗡𝗔𝗚𝗘𝗠𝗘𝗡𝗧:
Eliminate your CVE-noise with runtime reachability analysis
Prioritize critical vulnerabilities with runtime reachability context and threat intelligence, reduce CVE-Related Work by 90%+

𝗞𝗨𝗕𝗘𝗥𝗡𝗘𝗧𝗘𝗦 𝗦𝗘𝗖𝗨𝗥𝗜𝗧𝗬 [𝗞𝗦𝗣𝗠]:
Secure your registries, clusters and images
Reveal, prioritize, and remediate security issues and automate compliance for clusters, containers and workloads.

𝗖𝗟𝗢𝗨𝗗 𝗦𝗘𝗖𝗨𝗥𝗜𝗧𝗬 [𝗖𝗦𝗣𝗠]:
Minimize your cloud attack surface
Detect, contextualize, and remediate cloud risks across clouds, powered by runtime insights with agentless scanning.

𝗪𝗛𝗢 𝗪𝗘 𝗔𝗥𝗘:
ARMO, the creator and maintainer of Kubescape, the world’s fastest-growing and most adopted open-source Kubernetes security project (an official CNCF Project), is connecting the dots between Cloud, Kubernetes, container, and application security all with runtime insights. ARMO provides complete protection for your cloud applications with the first Behavioral Cloud Detection and Response (CADR) solution. Discover a fully explainable & traceable runtime security story spanning the entire cloud security stack.
companyResolutionResult.name: ARMO

organizationUrnResolutionResult.headquartersLocation: Be'er Sheva, South District, Israel
organizationUrnResolutionResult.description:
Ben-Gurion University of the Negev is one of Israel’s leading research universities and among the world leaders in many fields. 

It has around 20,000 students and 4,000 faculty members in the Faculties of Engineering Sciences; Health Sciences; Natural Sciences; the Pinchas Sapir Faculty of Humanities and Social Sciences; the Guilford Glazer Faculty of Business and Management; the Joyce and Irving Goldman School of Medicine; the Kreitman School of Advanced Graduate Studies; and the Albert Katz International School for Desert Studies. More than 100,000 alumni play important roles in all areas of research and development, industry, health care, the economy, society, culture and education in Israel.

The University has three main campuses: The Marcus Family Campus in Beer-Sheva; the research campus at Sede Boqer and the Eilat Campus, and is home to national and multi-disciplinary research institutes: the National Institute for Biotechnology in the Negev; the National Institute of Solar Energy; the Ilse Katz Institute for Nanoscale Science and Technology; the Jacob Blaustein Institutes for Desert Research; the Ben-Gurion Research Institute for the Study of Israel & Zionism, and Heksherim - The Research Institute for Jewish and Israeli Literature and Culture.
organizationUrnResolutionResult.name: Ben-Gurion University of the Negev

organizationUrnResolutionResult.headquartersLocation: Haifa, Haifa District, Israel
organizationUrnResolutionResult.description:
Approximately 36 years before Israel declared independence, a ceremony took place on the barren slopes of Mount Carmel near the Port of Haifa, which was then occupied by the Ottoman Empire. Unknown to the Haifa community witnessing the event, this would be a milestone in history. This first cornerstone embodied an implausible vision of creating a world-class institute of scientific and technological education in the Holy Land.
The story of the “Technikum” – the original German name of the Technion – is a tale of the century. The second industrial revolution created the printing presses and communications infrastructure allowing Jews scattered around the globe to organize in face of rising anti-Semitism. As Jews were often barred from technical education, the establishment of a technical school was a first priority to rebuilding a Jewish homeland. The Technion was to become a unique worldwide university that would precede, create, shape, and protect a modern state.
The cornerstone laid on April 11, 1912, set in motion a century of progress responding to national and global needs. Technion – Israel Institute of Technology would grow rapidly, becoming a global pioneer in fields such as biotechnology, stem cell research, space, computer science, nanotechnology, and energy. 
Four Technion professors have won Nobel Prizes. As it approaches its centennial in 2025 – 100 years after the first class took place – Technion City is a thriving world center of trailblazing excellence in research and teaching for the benefit of humanity, with 18 faculties, more than 14,000 students, 135 academic programs, and 60 research institutes. Since the Technion opened its doors, more than 90,000 Technion alumni have played a huge part in creating and leading the ‘Startup Nation,’ impacting the lives of millions of people worldwide.
organizationUrnResolutionResult.name: Technion - Israel Institute of Technology
